seoTitle: How to Log In to Coursera With a University ID
metaDescription: Sign in to Coursera through your university's institutional access, with the login steps and common troubleshooting tips for students and educators.
intro: If your university partners with Coursera, you can log in using your university ID to unlock institutional access to courses and specializations. This covers entering your credentials, navigating the institutional login flow, and troubleshooting common access issues.

steps.heading: Go to Coursera's institutional login option
steps.body: Find the login path meant for users signing in through a university or institution rather than a standard personal account.

steps.heading: Enter your university credentials
steps.body: Use the login details provided by your university to authenticate through its access system.

steps.heading: Complete any additional verification steps
steps.body: Follow through any extra prompts your university's login system requires to finish signing in.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common login issues
steps.body: If you can't get in, confirm you're using the correct institutional login method rather than a personal Coursera account.

faq.question: What is institutional access on Coursera?
faq.answer: It's a way for students and staff affiliated with a partnering university to log in and access courses through their university credentials.

faq.question: What if my university ID login doesn't work?
faq.answer: Confirm you're using the specific institutional login path rather than the general Coursera sign-in, since they're handled differently.

faq.question: Is institutional access free for students?
faq.answer: Access and pricing depend on the agreement between your university and Coursera, so check with your institution for specifics.

faq.question: Can educators use university ID login too?
faq.answer: Yes, this login path is designed for both students and educators affiliated with a partnering institution.
